blocks.text: Amounts recognized in Accumulated other comprehensive income (loss) at March 31, 2019 and 2018 consist of the
Although not reflected in the table above, the tax effect on the pension balances was $2.4 million and $2.3 million as
The components of net periodic benefit (income) costs for the fiscal years ended March 31, 2019, 2018 and 2017 are

_______________________________________________________________________________ (1) The Company has elected to use the actual fair value of plan assets as the market-related value of assets in the determination of the expected return on plan assets. (2) Actuarial gains and losses are amortized using a corridor approach. The total unrecognized amount of cumulative actuarial gain or loss in excess of 10% of the greater of the market value of assets or the projected benefit obligation is amortized over the average remaining service of active employees/lifetime of plan participants for plans with no active participants.
All of the amounts in the table above, other than net service cost, were recorded in the line item “Other (income)
expense, net” in the Consolidated Statements of Operations.
The estimated amounts related to pensions that will be amortized from accumulated other comprehensive income into

net periodic benefit costs in fiscal year 2020 are actuarial losses of $0.5 million, and prior service costs of $0.1 million. The estimated amounts related to other benefits that will be amortized from accumulated other comprehensive income into net periodic benefit costs in fiscal year 2020 are actuarial gains of $0.1 million.
